title: phaca subcinerea
intro: Phaca subcinerea, also known as the Indian clover or sweet clover, is a perennial legume native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. Phaca subcinerea can be prepared into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
